LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS QWEST COMMUNICATIONS, AND, DENNIS SHERWOOD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED FOR FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, DISCRIMINATION. NO EVIDENCE THAT ADVERSE ACTION WAS TAKEN BECAUSE OF PLAINTIFF'S GENDER. DEFENDANT OFFERS A NONDISCRIMINATORY REASON FOR THE CONDUCT (TO BRING THE QUOTA IN LINE WITH BRANCH EXPECTATIONS) AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THIS IS A MERE PRETEXT. MOTION DENIED FOR SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, HARASSMENT. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER CONDUCT SEVERE AND PERVASIVE. (MORAN DECLARATION.) TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER SHERWOOD'S CONDUCT WAS SUCH TO CREATE A HOSTILE WORKING ENVIRONMENT. MOTION DENIED FOR THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, FAILURE TO PREVENT HARASSMENT. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT'S PROCEDURE SUFFICIENT TO ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S HARASSMENT CONCERNS. MOTION DENIED FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER WORKING CONDITIONS INTOLERABLE DUE TO THE TREATMENT SHE RECEIVED FROM SHERWOOD AND QWEST'S DISREGARD OF HER COMPLAINT. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO MAKE IMMEDIATE PAYMENT OF WAGES UPON DISCHARGE AND WAITING TIME PENALTIES. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT PLAINTIFF WAS PAID ALL THE COMPENSATION SHE WAS ENTITLED TO UNDER THE COMPENSATION AGREEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE HELD, CASE NOT SETTLED. TRIAL DATE IS CONFIRMED. JUDGE: GAIL DEKREON, CLERK:M. SISON, NOT REPORTED, DEPT 306.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS QWEST COMMUNICATIONS, and, DENNIS SHERWOOD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: William Gaus, Esq. of Dillingham & Murphy LLP (415) 397-2700, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Otis McGee, Jr. Esq. of Sheppard Mullin (415) 434-9100 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication granted for first cause of action, discrimination. No evidence that adverse action was taken because of plaintiff's gender. Defendant offers a nondiscriminatory reason for the conduct (to bring the quota in line with branch expectations) and plaintiff fails to establish this is a mere pretext. Motion denied for second cause of action, harassment. Triable issue of fact whether conduct severe and pervasive. (Moran declaration.) Triable issue of fact whether Sherwood's conduct was such to create a hostile working environment. Motion denied for third cause of action, failure to prevent harassment. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant's procedure sufficient to address plaintiff's harassment concerns. Motion denied for the fourth cause of action, termination in violation of public policy. Triable issue of fact raised whether working conditions intolerable due to the treatment she received from Sherwood and Qwest's disregard of her complaint. Summary adjudication granted as to fifth cause of action for failure to make immediate payment of wages upon discharge and waiting time penalties. No triable issue of fact that plaintiff was paid all the compensation she was entitled to under the compensation agreement. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
